symbols.name: \nu
symbols.def: Common dominating measure for the full-data classes.

symbols.name: \mu
symbols.def: Common dominating measure for observed-data laws.

symbols.name: e_P
symbols.def: A fixed Borel version of \(P(A=1\mid X=\cdot)\).

symbols.name: m_{P,a}
symbols.def: A fixed Borel version of \(E_P[Y\mid A=a,X=\cdot]\), for \(a\in\{0,1\}\).

symbols.name: p_{j,-}
symbols.def: Lower endpoint of the exact tie-mass band.

symbols.name: p_{j,+}
symbols.def: Upper endpoint of the exact tie-mass band.

symbols.name: \delta_j
symbols.def: Uniform contrast gap in a separated stratum.

symbols.name: \zeta_j
symbols.def: Declared smoothness, margin, tie-band, and separation index.

symbols.name: \mathcal Z
symbols.def: Set of indices in the two explicitly displayed geometry branches in \(\mathrm{def:admissible-indices}\).

symbols.name: h^\circ_{j,n}
symbols.def: Largest numerical minimizer of \(h\mapsto h^{\alpha_j+1}+\rho_{j,n}^2/h\) over \(\mathcal H_{j,n}\).

symbols.name: \eta
symbols.def: A pair \(\eta=(m_0,m_1)\) of candidate outcome regressions.

symbols.name: \widehat m^{(j)}_{a,-k}
symbols.def: Clipped least-squares projection on a fixed boundary-corrected tensor-wavelet basis through level \(q_{j,n}\), trained on \(\{i\in I_k^c:A_i=a\}\); if that set has fewer observations than the basis dimension, it is identically zero.

symbols.name: \widehat j
symbols.def: Label selected by the deterministic rule in \(\mathrm{def:interval-construction}\).

symbols.name: L_n
symbols.def: Lower endpoint of a generic interval.

symbols.name: U_n
symbols.def: Upper endpoint of a generic interval.

symbols.name: \mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)
symbols.def: Sequences of sample-measurable, almost surely finite connected closed intervals \(C_n=[L_n,U_n]\) with \(L_n\le U_n\) and \(\liminf_{n\to\infty}\inf_{P\in\mathcal U}P^n\{V(P)\in C_n\}\ge1-\gamma\).

symbols.name: q_j
symbols.def: For \(\bar p_j>0\), a nonzero \(C^\infty\) function supported in \(\{x:x_1<\bar p_j\}\), with \(\lVert q_j\rVert_{C^{s_j}(\mathcal X)}\le1\).

novel.justification: A predeclared band represents plausible prevalence of exact response ties and is a property of the law rather than of a fitted policy.
A fixed clinically meaningful efficacy gap defines a regular stratum before the outcome sample is observed.

definitions.name: \(\mathcal Z\) and the \(C^s\) convention
definitions.construction: Write \(s=r+\tau\) with \(r=\lfloor s\rfloor\) and \(\tau\in[0,1)\); \(C^s(\mathcal X)\) consists of functions with bounded continuous derivatives through order \(r\), whose order-\(r\) derivatives are \(\tau\)-Hölder when \(\tau>0\). The set \(\mathcal Z\) contains exactly indices with \(L_j\ge B\), \(0\le p_{j,-}\le p_{j,+}\le1\), and either: nonseparated geometry \(\delta_j=0,p_{j,+}<1,1/\alpha_j\ge s_j\) if \(s_j\notin\mathbb N\), \(1/\alpha_j>s_j\) if \(s_j\in\mathbb N\), and \(C_j\kappa_j^{\alpha_j}\ge1\); or separated geometry \(0<\delta_j\le\delta_{\max},p_{j,-}=p_{j,+}=0,\alpha_j>0,C_j\delta_j^{\alpha_j}\ge1\).

definitions.name: \(\mathcal F_j\) and \(\mathcal P_j\)
definitions.construction: For each \(j\in\{1,\ldots,J\}\) with \(\zeta_j\in\mathcal Z\), \(\mathcal F_j=\{F\ll\nu:P=T_\#F\text{ satisfies }\mathrm{ass:consistency},\mathrm{ass:exchangeability},\mathrm{ass:randomization},\mathrm{ass:design-density},\mathrm{ass:outcome-noise},\mathrm{ass:holder-regression},\mathrm{ass:interior-means},\mathrm{ass:tie-mass-band},\mathrm{ass:margin},\mathrm{ass:separated-gap}\}\), and \(\mathcal P_j=\{T_\#F:F\in\mathcal F_j\}\).

definitions.name: feasible full-data members
definitions.construction: For separated \(j\), take \(X\sim\operatorname{Unif}(\mathcal X)\), \(A\sim\operatorname{Bernoulli}(1/2)\) independently, and \(m_0=-\delta_j/2,m_1=\delta_j/2\). For nonseparated \(j\), use \(m_0=-\kappa_jf_j/2,m_1=\kappa_jf_j/2\). In both cases, conditionally independently draw \(Y(a)\in\{-B,B\}\) with \(P_F\{Y(a)=B\mid X=x\}=(B+m_a(x))/(2B)\).

definitions.name: finite candidate score family
definitions.construction: Fix \(I_0,I_1\). For each \(j\), use the displayed clipped wavelet learner at level \(q_{j,n}\), evaluate the score only at \(h^\circ_{j,n}\), and compute \(\widehat V_j,\widehat\sigma_j,b_j(h^\circ_{j,n},n),\bar b_n,w_j\) by their symbol-table formulas. The grid is used solely to define \(h^\circ_{j,n}\), so the studentized family has exactly \(J\) members.

definitions.name: \(C_n^\star\)
definitions.construction: Let \(\mathcal R_n=\{j:|\widehat V_j-\widehat V_\ell|\le2(w_j+w_\ell)\text{ for every }\ell\text{ with }w_\ell>w_j\}\). It is nonempty because each largest-width candidate belongs to it. Let \(\widehat j\) minimize \(w_j\) on \(\mathcal R_n\), breaking ties by the smallest label, and set \(C_n^\star=[\widehat V_{\widehat j}-2w_{\widehat j},\widehat V_{\widehat j}+2w_{\widehat j}]\).

definitions.name: testing modulus and sequence coefficient
definitions.construction: For every positive deterministic \(a=(a_n)_n\), use \(\omega^{\mathrm{test}}_{j,n}\) and \(\mathfrak R^{\mathrm{asy}}_j(a)\) exactly as defined in the symbol table; honesty is imposed on the entire sequence \((C_n)_n\), not separately at each \(n\).

definitions.name: tie-preserving local submodel
definitions.construction: For nonseparated \(j\) with \(\bar p_j>0\), start from its member in \(\mathrm{def:feasible-members}\). For sufficiently small \(t>0\), replace both regressions on the support of \(q_j\) by \(m_{j,t,a}=m_{j,0,a}+tq_j\), retain the laws of \(X,A\), and draw conditionally independent Rademacher outcomes with these means. Let \(F_{j,t}\) be the resulting full-data law and \(P_{j,t}=T_\#F_{j,t}\).

statements.statement: For every nonseparated \(\zeta_j\in\mathcal Z\), \(f_j\in C^{s_j}(\mathcal X)\), \(P_X\{f_j(X)=0\}=\bar p_j\), and \(P_X\{0<\kappa_jf_j(X)\le u\}\le(u/\kappa_j)^{\alpha_j}\). For every separated \(\zeta_j\in\mathcal Z\), the constant contrast \(\Delta_P=\delta_j\) obeys its margin and tie restrictions. Consequently \(\mathrm{def:feasible-members}\) yields \(F_j^0\in\mathcal F_j\) in both branches.
statements.justification: Each declared stratum requires a compatible dominated full-data member.
statements.gap: Optimal-value inference papers do not audit nonemptiness of every margin--tie band in a union-honesty model.
statements.consumer: It ensures that the union and testing modulus are not vacuous.

statements.statement: For every \(j,a,k\) and all sufficiently large \(n\), \(\sup_{P\in\mathcal P_j}P^n\{\lVert\widehat m^{(j)}_{a,-k}-m_{P,a}\rVert_{L^2(P_X)}>M_j\rho_{j,n}\}\le2n^{-2}\), and the zero fallback has probability at most \(2n^{-2}\), uniformly over \(P\in\mathcal P_j\).
statements.justification: The named, clipped wavelet learner supplies a law-primitive uniform nuisance guarantee and remains measurable on undersized folds.
statements.gap: Exceptional-law analyses usually posit value-regret control rather than derive a uniform learner bound from Hölder primitives.
statements.consumer: It supplies the nuisance component of the displayed bias envelope.

statements.statement: The measurable sequence \((C_n^\star)_n\) belongs to \(\mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)\). For every \(j\), \(\sup_{P\in\mathcal P_j}E_{P^n}[\operatorname{length}(C_n^\star)]\le D_{j,\gamma}[n^{-1/2}+r_{j,n}]\) for all sufficiently large \(n\), and hence \(\mathfrak R^{\mathrm{asy}}_j((n^{-1/2}+r_{j,n})_n)<\infty\).
statements.justification: This is the complete finite-candidate construction whose union honesty and length bound require a new argument.
statements.gap: Wu--Wang, Xu--Guo, Whitehouse--Chen--Austern--Syrgkanis, and Shi--Lu--Song provide nonregular optimal-value procedures. Armstrong--Kolesar give bias-aware honest intervals under predeclared smoothness restrictions and warn against honest data-driven smoothness adaptation. None establishes one connected interval uniformly asymptotically honest over this fixed finite causal tie--margin--smoothness union with the stated sequence-level adaptation criterion.
statements.consumer: It is the binary core for an optimized rilpivirine-versus-efavirenz value interval after coarsening nuisances are added.

statements.statement: For every \((C_n)_n\in\mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)\) and sequences \(P_n\in\mathcal P_j,Q_n\in\mathcal U\) with \(0<|V(P_n)-V(Q_n)|\) and \(H^2(P_n,Q_n)\le1/(8n)\), \(\liminf_{n\to\infty}E_{P_n^n}[\operatorname{length}(C_n)]/|V(P_n)-V(Q_n)|\ge1/2-2\gamma\). If \(\omega^{\mathrm{test}}_{j,n}>0\) eventually, near-maximizing testing pairs imply \(\liminf_{n\to\infty}\sup_{P\in\mathcal P_j}E_{P^n}[\operatorname{length}(C_n)]/\omega^{\mathrm{test}}_{j,n}\ge1/2-2\gamma\), and therefore \(\mathfrak R^{\mathrm{asy}}_j((\omega^{\mathrm{test}}_{j,n})_n)\ge1/2-2\gamma\).
statements.justification: Product Hellinger distance is at most \(1/8\), so transfer of the \(Q_n\)-coverage event gives the stated two-point constant; near-maximizers avoid both nonattainment and zero denominators.
statements.gap: Cai and Low derive ordered-modulus bounds for Gaussian linear functionals; this is the causal max-functional sequence analogue.
statements.consumer: It gives a lower bound every union-honest interval sequence must obey.

statements.statement: For nonseparated \(j\) with \(\bar p_j>0\), sufficiently small \(t>0\) gives \(F_{j,t}\in\mathcal F_j\), \(V(P_{j,t})-V(P_{j,0})=tQ_{j,1}\), and \(H^2(P_{j,t},P_{j,0})\le t^2Q_{j,2}/B^2\). Thus \(t_n\) yields same-stratum separation \(BQ_{j,1}/\sqrt{8nQ_{j,2}}\) at testing radius \(1/(8n)\).
statements.justification: A common-mean perturbation preserves both the blip and its tie mass.
statements.gap: Luedtke and van der Laan identify exceptional-law nonregularity but do not calculate this tie-preserving causal testing submodel.
statements.consumer: With the testing theorem it yields a root-\(n\) lower-bound component at positive tie mass.

statements.statement: Conditional on \(\mathrm{conj:uniform-honesty-upper-length}\), separated strata have candidate \(O(n^{-1/2})\) length whenever \(s_j/(2s_j+d)>(\alpha_j+2)/[4(\alpha_j+1)]\). For nonseparated strata, \(r_{j,n}\asymp(\log n/n)^{2s_j(\alpha_j+1)/((2s_j+d)(\alpha_j+2))}\); positive tie mass additionally has the root-\(n\) lower component in \(\mathrm{lem:tie-plateau-submodel}\).
statements.justification: The display is the construction's explicit two-geometry candidate diagram, not a proved minimax assertion.
statements.gap: Existing work does not formulate this union-honest expected-length comparison across declared separation and tie geometries.
statements.consumer: It identifies the candidate precision regimes for biomarker strata.

statements.statement: The index set contains a separated example \(d=1,s_j=2,\alpha_j=1,0<\delta_j\le\delta_{\max},p_{j,-}=p_{j,+}=0,C_j\ge\delta_j^{-1}\), for which \(2/5>3/8\) and \(r_{j,n}=o(n^{-1/2})\); and a nonseparated example \(d=1,s_j=1,\alpha_j=1/2,\delta_j=0,p_{j,-}=p_{j,+}=1/2,C_j\ge\kappa_j^{-1/2}\), for which \(f_j=(x_1-1/2)_+^2\in C^1(\mathcal X)\), the variance and outcome bounds hold, and each \(\mathcal H_{j,n}\) is nonempty.
statements.justification: Both geometry branches must be visibly feasible under their distinct restrictions.
statements.gap: The literature does not supply this compatibility audit for a finite union of tie and separation strata.
statements.consumer: It prevents importing the separated root-\(n\) calculation into the nonseparated branch.

statements.statement: For which positive benchmark sequences \(a\) and ordered pairs \((j,k)\) can one \((C_n)_n\in\mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)\) make \(\mathfrak R^{\mathrm{asy}}_j(a)\) finite at both stratumwise rates, and for which pairs does the ordered testing modulus force a strict adaptation penalty?
statements.justification: Sequence-level validity and a candidate upper bound do not identify the achievable adaptation diagram.
statements.gap: Cai--Low give ordered-modulus theory for linear Gaussian functionals and Armstrong--Kolesar show why honest smoothness adaptation needs explicit bias control; no cited optimal-value article characterizes the ordered expected-length diagram jointly over predeclared causal margin, tie-mass, and nuisance-smoothness strata.
statements.consumer: The answer determines whether one clinical interval can adapt across competing biomarker-response geometries.

sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: Two deterministic folds use clipped tensor-wavelet least squares at integer level \(q_{j,n}\), with the identically zero fallback on undersized treatment folds.

estimand_functional: \(C_n^\star=[\widehat V_{\widehat j}-2w_{\widehat j},\widehat V_{\widehat j}+2w_{\widehat j}],\quad r_{j,n}=\min_{h\in\mathcal H_{j,n}}\{h^{\alpha_j+1}+\rho_{j,n}^2/h\}\)

bibliography.citation: A. R. Luedtke and M. J. van der Laan (2016). Statistical inference for the mean outcome under a possibly non-unique optimal treatment strategy. \textit{Annals of Statistics} 44, 713--742. doi:10.1214/15-AOS1384.
P. Wu and L. Wang (2021). Inference for optimal treatment regimes. \textit{Biometrics} 77. doi:10.1111/biom.13337.
S. Xu and X. Guo (2026). Optimal inference of the mean outcome under optimal treatment regime. \textit{arXiv:2509.09773}.
J. Whitehouse, Q. Chen, M. Austern, and V. Syrgkanis (2025). Inference on optimal policy values and other irregular functionals via softmax smoothing. \textit{arXiv:2507.11780}.

C. Shi, W. Lu, and R. Song (2020). Breaking the curse of nonregularity with subagging: Inference of the mean outcome under optimal treatment regimes. \textit{Journal of Machine Learning Research} 21(176), 1--67.
T. T. Cai and M. G. Low (2004). An adaptation theory for nonparametric confidence intervals. \textit{Annals of Statistics} 32, 1805--1840. doi:10.1214/009053604000000049.
T. B. Armstrong and M. Kolesar (2020). Simple and honest confidence intervals in nonparametric regression. \textit{Quantitative Economics} 11, 1--39. doi:10.3982/QE1199.
J.-Y. Audibert and A. B. Tsybakov (2007). Fast learning rates for plug-in classifiers. \textit{Annals of Statistics} 35, 608--633. doi:10.1214/009053606000001217.

W. Liu, Z. Zhang, L. Nie, and G. Soon (2017). A case study in personalized medicine: Rilpivirine versus efavirenz for treatment-naive HIV patients. \textit{Journal of the American Statistical Association} 112, 1381--1392. doi:10.1080/01621459.2017.1280404.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: The article gives smoothed robust asymptotically accurate inference for an optimal-regime parameter and its value.
The preprint develops adaptive smoothing and an asymptotic-variance bound within a robust asymptotically linear unbiased estimator class.
The preprint gives softmax-smoothed optimal-policy-value inference and claims root-\(n\) convergence without parametric outcome-regression restrictions or strong margin assumptions.
The article characterizes exceptional-law nonregularity and develops optimal-value inference under sufficient conditions.
The article develops subagging confidence intervals for nonregular optimal-value inference and compares their average length with sample splitting.
The article derives ordered-modulus expected-length lower bounds for Gaussian linear functionals.
The article constructs bias-aware honest confidence intervals under predeclared smoothness restrictions and emphasizes the obstruction to honest data-driven smoothness adaptation.

tldr: The binary optimal-policy value is nonsmooth at treatment-response ties. Before sampling, the proposal fixes a finite union of explicit dominated full-data causal strata, every stratum tuple, the deterministic grids and candidate rule, outcome and design bounds, randomization, and \(\gamma\). The data enter only through cross-fitted nuisance fits, score, variance, and bias-envelope evaluation, and the stated measurable candidate selection. The interval's uniform asymptotic honesty over that entire predeclared union and its candidate expected-length bound are conjectural. A separately stated product-Hellinger two-point theorem and tie-preserving submodel provide lower-bound machinery; rate sharpness and the ordered adaptation diagram remain open.

project_justification.gap: Existing nonregular optimal-value procedures do not settle whether one connected interval is uniformly asymptotically honest over a fixed finite predeclared union of tie, margin, and smoothness strata, nor its sequence-level ordered expected-length cost. Armstrong--Kolesar establish bias-aware honesty under fixed smoothness restrictions and warn that this does not generally extend to unrestricted data-driven smoothness adaptation.
project_justification.niche: The kernel fixes the finite causal union before sampling and separates its explicit measurable candidate construction, valid causal testing lower bound, and unresolved adaptation claim from adaptation over an unknown continuum of smoothness classes.
project_justification.fill: It supplies dominated full-data classes, admissible and nonempty index branches, a finite-candidate interval, a named uniform nuisance learner, and a sequence-level product-Hellinger lower-bound target; any adaptation is confined to the declared union and remains conjectural.

related_work: Luedtke and van der Laan \cite{LuedtkeVanderLaan2016} characterize exceptional-law nonregularity through pathwise differentiability and motivate the tie boundary. Wu and Wang \cite{WuWang2021} provide smoothed robust and resampling-based inference for an optimal-regime parameter and value, and are methodological background only. Xu and Guo \cite{XuGuo2026} develop adaptive smoothing and an asymptotic-variance bound within a robust asymptotically linear unbiased estimator class, which informs the finite-grid device. Whitehouse, Chen, Austern, and Syrgkanis \cite{WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025} give softmax-smoothed optimal-policy-value inference and claim root-\(n\) convergence without parametric outcome-regression restrictions or strong margin assumptions. Shi, Lu, and Song \cite{ShiLuSong2020} use subagging for nonregular optimal-value confidence intervals and compare average lengths. Cai and Low \cite{CaiLow2004} develop ordered moduli for expected-length adaptation of linear Gaussian functionals. Armstrong and Kolesar \cite{ArmstrongKolesar2020} construct bias-aware honest intervals under predeclared smoothness restrictions and emphasize the obstruction to honest data-driven smoothness adaptation. None of these papers establishes one connected interval uniformly asymptotically honest over the present fixed finite causal union of tie, margin, and smoothness strata together with its sequence-level stratumwise expected-length and testing-modulus adaptation diagram. The present finite-union formulation does not evade the Armstrong--Kolesar warning: \(J\), every stratum tuple, the deterministic grids and candidate rule, outcome and design bounds, randomization, and \(\gamma\) are fixed before sampling; data are used only for the defined cross-fitted nuisance fits, score, variance, bias-envelope evaluation, and measurable candidate selection, and the resulting adaptation claim remains conjectural. Audibert and Tsybakov \cite{AudibertTsybakov2007} supply Hölder-margin geometry, and Liu, Zhang, Nie, and Soon \cite{LiuZhangNieSoon2017} supply the rilpivirine-versus-efavirenz consumer setting under monotone coarsening.
technical_internal_limitation: The finite-candidate interval is a conjectural upper construction. Neither its uniform asymptotic coverage over the fixed finite union, its expected-length bound, nor attainment of the ordered testing modulus is encoded as an assumption or asserted as a theorem. The construction does not claim honest adaptation over an unrestricted or unknown smoothness class.
honest_scope: The settled theorem target is the sequence-level product-Hellinger two-point lower bound, and the tie-plateau construction is a separate lemma target. The construction's uniform asymptotic honesty over the entire fixed finite predeclared union, its expected-length upper bound, and the exact feasible-versus-penalized adaptation pairs remain open. Every stratum tuple, deterministic grid and candidate rule, outcome and design bound, randomization, and \(\gamma\) is fixed before sampling; estimated propensities, monotone coarsening, unrestricted unions, and unknown-smoothness adaptation are outside the model.
